analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet commemorates Russia’s Day of Solidarity in the Fight Against Terrorism, recalling the 2004 Beslan school attack and mourning the victims, mostly children. Evaluation per narrative: RU1 (West immoral/hostile) – Not promoted; the tweet does not mention the West or assign blame to Western actors. RU2 (Russia leads an alternative system) – Not promoted; no reference to international systems or multilateralism. RU3 (Russia is a benevolent partner) – Partially relevant in tone of solidarity, but the tweet focuses on remembrance of a national tragedy, not on Russia’s partnership, mediation, or external benevolence; therefore not sufficiently supported. RU4 (Russian history is admirable) – Not promoted; this is a somber remembrance, not a celebration of historical achievements or contributions. RU5 (Russia at the forefront) – Not promoted; no claims of success or leadership. RU6 (Russia’s culture/nature/heritage) – Not promoted; the content is not about culture or heritage in the promotional sense. Conclusion: The tweet does not clearly promote any listed strategic narratives.
